-{"id":"bd-5wc7","title":"Audit README inventory counts against the current tree and define a counting method","description":"## Background
README badges and narrative inventory claims (API/header/test counts) must match reality. The current README claims don't match actual counts. This bead establishes the authoritative counting method so all downstream beads (bd-cc14, bd-smx9) use consistent numbers.

## Current discrepancies (measured 2026-04-07)
| Metric | README Claims | Actual | Method |
|--------|--------------|--------|--------|
| Source files | 123 | 123 | `find src/ -name '*.c' \| wc -l` |
| Public headers | 122 | 125 | `find include/asx -name '*.h' \| wc -l` |
| ASX_API declarations | 1,359 | 1,374 | `grep -r ASX_API include/ --glob '*.h' \| count` |
| Header families | 37 | count TBD | Subdirectories of include/asx/ with at least one .h |
| Test programs | 198-199 | 204 | `find tests/ -name '*.c' \| wc -l` (by category) |
| Examples | 14 | 16 | `ls examples/*.c \| wc -l` |

## Deliverables
1. Define the ONE canonical counting command for each metric
2. Document the method in a comment at the top of README.md (or in a tools/count_inventory.sh script)
3. Run each command and record the authoritative counts
4. Note: e2e tests have both .c driver files (20) and .sh harness scripts (27) — define which count as 'test programs'

## Why this matters
Multiple agents have independently counted different things and gotten different numbers. A single authoritative method prevents future drift.

## Files
- tools/count_inventory.sh (new, optional: script that outputs all counts)
- README.md (update counts once method is defined — done by bd-cc14)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"docs","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:04:50.428800025Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T04:17:25.254206953Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id": "bd-66l", "title": "EPIC: Hard Quality Gates and CI Enforcement", "description": "Background:
The plan defines non-negotiable gates. They must be automated and enforced to prevent regressions from entering mainline.

Goal:
Encode all required gates in CI with reproducible artifacts and clear fail reasons.

Gates include:
- portability, OOM semantics, fuzz parity
- embedded matrix, profile parity, semantic-delta budget
- HFT tail/jitter, automotive deadline/watchdog
- crash/restart replay continuity
- warning/static-analysis/bounded-proof gates

Why this serves users:
- transforms policy into enforceable guarantees
- makes trust scalable beyond individual reviewers.

Cross-cutting quality contract:
- Child tasks must define and link unit tests, e2e script coverage (when behavioral), and structured log/manifest artifacts or explicit rationale for non-applicability.
- Completion evidence must remain compatible with bd-66l.9 enforcement and traceability index expectations.", "notes": "Executed API ergonomics validation lane work (bd-56t.5 content) under this quality-gate epic: added make test-vignettes target, CI step+manifest wiring, tests/TEST.md vignette coverage docs, docs/API_ERGONOMICS_VALIDATION.md sign-off, and fixed vignette compile/runtime issues. Validation via rch: make test-vignettes/build/format-check/lint/test pass. Conformance now runs under rch with SEMANTIC_DELTA_ARTIFACT_DIR override support in tools/ci/run_conformance.sh; current remote conformance still fails on existing non-budgetable semantic-delta condition (fail=1, diff_records=1), not a permission failure.", "status": "closed", "priority": 0, "issue_type": "epic", "assignee": "IvoryGlacier", "created_at": "2026-02-27T00:47:42.390018086Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-03-01T05:30:49.559586930Z", "closed_at": "2026-03-01T05:30:49.559560691Z", "close_reason": "done", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["ci", "phase-10", "quality-gates"], "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "bd-66l", "depends_on_id": "bd-1md", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-02T04:37:11Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-66l", "depends_on_id": "bd-296", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-02T04:37:11Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-66l", "depends_on_id": "bd-2cw", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-02T04:37:11Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-66l", "depends_on_id": "bd-2n0", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-02T04:37:11Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-66l", "depends_on_id": "bd-hwb", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-02T04:37:11Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-66l", "depends_on_id": "bd-ix8", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-02T04:37:11Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-66l", "depends_on_id": "bd-j4m", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-02T04:37:11Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}]}
 {"id": "bd-66l.1", "title": "Implement CI workflow matrix for build, tests, conformance, profiles, fuzz, embedded, and perf", "description": "Background:
Quality policy is only real when encoded in CI. Workflow structure must mirror plan-gate structure.

Subtasks:
- Define jobs for check, unit-invariant, conformance, profile-parity, fuzz-parity, embedded-matrix, perf-tail-deadline, and e2e scenario packs.
- Ensure deterministic artifact naming and retention.
- Ensure failure output is actionable and machine-readable where possible.
- Enforce publication of test run manifests and first-failure summaries for each test lane.

Acceptance Criteria:
- CI runs all required jobs on expected triggers.
- Job outputs are sufficient for triage without rerunning everything locally.
- No critical gate is left as manual-only.
- Missing e2e artifacts/manifests fail the workflow.", "notes": "2026-02-28: Completed CI workflow hardening for lane manifests/first-failure summaries and deterministic artifact naming. Updated .github/workflows/ci.yml and perf.yml: added per-lane machine-readable manifest emission (build/ci-manifests/*.manifest.json), e2e run_manifest publication enforcement, conformance/profile summary presence checks, fuzz/embedded artifact validation, lint-docs in check lane, deterministic artifact names using github.run_id/run_attempt. Verification via rch offload: make format-check, lint, build, test, conformance, profile-parity all PASS.", "status": "closed", "priority": 0, "issue_type": "task", "assignee": "FrostyPrairie", "created_at": "2026-02-27T00:51:00.106194478Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-02-28T01:21:14.302625020Z", "closed_at": "2026-02-28T01:21:14.302597949Z", "close_reason": "Implemented CI/perf workflow matrix refinements, deterministic artifact naming, lane manifest + first-failure summaries, and manifest/artifact enforcement checks.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["ci", "gates", "workflows"], "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "bd-66l.1", "depends_on_id": "bd-1md.10", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-02T04:37:11Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-66l.1", "depends_on_id": "bd-1md.11", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-02T04:37:11Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-66l.1", "depends_on_id": "bd-1md.2", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-02T04:37:11Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-66l.1", "depends_on_id": "bd-66l", "type": "parent-child", "created_at": "2026-03-02T04:37:11Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-66l.1", "depends_on_id": "bd-ix8.3", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-02T04:37:11Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}]}

 {"id":"bd-6u8q","title":"Child regions: Implement real Q2 check in quiescence.c","description":"Replace the hardcoded Q2 conjunct at src/runtime/quiescence.c line 150:

BEFORE:
    /* Q2: all child regions closed (walking skeleton: always true) */
    out->q2_children_closed = 1;

AFTER:
    /* Q2: all child regions closed */
    out->q2_children_closed = (r->child_count == 0) ? 1 : 0;

This is a one-line semantic change but it's the core fix for the quiescence contract violation. With child_count properly maintained by the open_child/close tracking, this correctly reflects whether all children have reached CLOSED state.

## Files
- src/runtime/quiescence.c (modify: line 150)","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T13:59:44.069290149Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T20:31:02.603644945Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T20:31:02.603296349Z","close_reason":"Implemented real Q2 child-closure check in detailed quiescence report","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-6u8q","depends_on_id":"bd-877h","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T14:01:47.466979962Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

-{"id":"bd-76sl","title":"Makefile: fix make install to install ALL public header subdirectories","description":"## Problem
The current `make install` target only installs top-level headers:
```make
install -m 644 include/asx/*.h $(DESTDIR)$(PREFIX)/include/asx/
```

This misses ALL subdirectory headers:
- include/asx/core/*.h (19 headers)
- include/asx/runtime/*.h (28 headers)
- include/asx/channel/*.h
- include/asx/sync/*.h
- include/asx/codec/*.h
- include/asx/actor/*.h
- include/asx/platform/*.h
- ... and ~15 more subdirectories

A user who runs `make install` and then `#include <asx/asx.h>` will get compilation errors because the umbrella header includes subdirectory headers that weren't installed.

## What to fix
Update the install target to recursively install all public headers preserving directory structure:
```make
install: release
	install -d $(DESTDIR)$(PREFIX)/lib
	install -m 644 $(LIB_DIR)/libasx.a $(DESTDIR)$(PREFIX)/lib/
	cp -r include/asx $(DESTDIR)$(PREFIX)/include/
```
Or use a find-based approach to create all subdirectories and copy headers.

## Verification
After fix: `make install PREFIX=/tmp/asx-test` should populate all header subdirectories. Compile a test program against the installed headers to verify.

## Files
- Makefile (modify install target)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:14:02.558880533Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T04:26:29.062035124Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T04:26:29.061765884Z","close_reason":"Completed: install target now stages full public header tree and verified staged install","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-76sl","title":"Makefile: fix make install to install ALL public header subdirectories","description":"## Problem
The current `make install` target only installs top-level headers:
```make
install -m 644 include/asx/*.h $(DESTDIR)$(PREFIX)/include/asx/
```

This misses ALL subdirectory headers:
- include/asx/core/*.h (19 headers)
- include/asx/runtime/*.h (28 headers)
- include/asx/channel/*.h
- include/asx/sync/*.h
- include/asx/codec/*.h
- include/asx/actor/*.h
- include/asx/platform/*.h
- ... and ~15 more subdirectories

A user who runs `make install` and then `#include <asx/asx.h>` will get compilation errors because the umbrella header includes subdirectory headers that weren't installed.

## What to fix
Update the install target to recursively install all public headers preserving directory structure:
```make
install: release
	install -d $(DESTDIR)$(PREFIX)/lib
	install -m 644 $(LIB_DIR)/libasx.a $(DESTDIR)$(PREFIX)/lib/
	cp -r include/asx $(DESTDIR)$(PREFIX)/include/
```
Or use a find-based approach to create all subdirectories and copy headers.

## Verification
After fix: `make install PREFIX=/tmp/asx-test` should populate all header subdirectories. Compile a test program against the installed headers to verify.

-{"id":"bd-howq","title":"Fix test_profile_compat to work under all profiles, not just CORE","description":"## Problem
tests/unit/runtime/test_profile_compat.c line 26-30 has:
```c
TEST(profile_active_is_core) {
    asx_profile_id id = asx_profile_active();
    ASSERT_EQ((int)id, (int)ASX_PROFILE_ID_CORE);
}
```

This test FAILS under any non-CORE profile (POSIX, WIN32, etc.) because it hardcodes the expected profile as CORE. When running `make test PROFILE=POSIX`, this test causes a spurious failure.

## What to fix
Replace the hardcoded assertion with one that checks the COMPILED profile:
```c
TEST(profile_active_matches_compiled) {
    asx_profile_id id = asx_profile_active();
#if defined(ASX_PROFILE_POSIX)
    ASSERT_EQ((int)id, (int)ASX_PROFILE_ID_POSIX);
#elif defined(ASX_PROFILE_WIN32)
    ASSERT_EQ((int)id, (int)ASX_PROFILE_ID_WIN32);
#elif defined(ASX_PROFILE_FREESTANDING)
    ASSERT_EQ((int)id, (int)ASX_PROFILE_ID_FREESTANDING);
/* ... etc for each profile ... */
#else
    ASSERT_EQ((int)id, (int)ASX_PROFILE_ID_CORE);
#endif
}
```

Also rename the test function and its RUN_TEST call from `profile_active_is_core` to `profile_active_matches_compiled`.

## Files
- tests/unit/runtime/test_profile_compat.c (modify)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:14:27.893180461Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T03:14:27.893180461Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-howq","title":"Fix test_profile_compat to work under all profiles, not just CORE","description":"## Problem
tests/unit/runtime/test_profile_compat.c line 26-30 has:
```c
TEST(profile_active_is_core) {
    asx_profile_id id = asx_profile_active();
    ASSERT_EQ((int)id, (int)ASX_PROFILE_ID_CORE);
}
```

This test FAILS under any non-CORE profile (POSIX, WIN32, etc.) because it hardcodes the expected profile as CORE. When running `make test PROFILE=POSIX`, this test causes a spurious failure.

## What to fix
Replace the hardcoded assertion with one that checks the COMPILED profile:
```c
TEST(profile_active_matches_compiled) {
    asx_profile_id id = asx_profile_active();
#if defined(ASX_PROFILE_POSIX)
    ASSERT_EQ((int)id, (int)ASX_PROFILE_ID_POSIX);
#elif defined(ASX_PROFILE_WIN32)
    ASSERT_EQ((int)id, (int)ASX_PROFILE_ID_WIN32);
#elif defined(ASX_PROFILE_FREESTANDING)
    ASSERT_EQ((int)id, (int)ASX_PROFILE_ID_FREESTANDING);
/* ... etc for each profile ... */
#else
    ASSERT_EQ((int)id, (int)ASX_PROFILE_ID_CORE);
#endif
}
```

Also rename the test function and its RUN_TEST call from `profile_active_is_core` to `profile_active_matches_compiled`.

 {"id":"bd-hsxb","title":"Win32: install mingw-w64 and verify cross-compilation","description":"## Background
The Win32 platform adapter (src/platform/win32/hooks.c) was implemented in the current session with all hooks: QPC clock, BCryptGenRandom entropy, WSAPoll/SleepEx reactor, CreateThread blocking pool, stderr+OutputDebugString log, and asx_win32_hooks_install() export function. The header include/asx/platform/win32.h was created. The Makefile build-win32 target was added.

However, this code has NEVER been compiled because mingw-w64 is not installed on this system. The code compiles correctly on CORE and POSIX profiles (since it's behind #ifdef ASX_PROFILE_WIN32).

## What to do
1. Install mingw-w64: sudo apt install gcc-mingw-w64-x86-64
2. Run: make build-win32
3. Fix any compilation errors
4. Verify the CI workflow handles Win32 cross-compilation

## Why this matters
The Win32 adapter is complete but UNPROVEN. Cross-compilation is the minimum bar for a 'working' Win32 adapter since we can't run Windows binaries on this Linux host.

## Files
- src/platform/win32/hooks.c (fix if needed)
- include/asx/platform/win32.h (fix if needed)
- Makefile (fix build-win32 target if needed)","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:13:50.147262930Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T03:13:50.147262930Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id": "bd-hwb", "title": "EPIC: Core Semantics and Guarantee Substitution in ANSI C/C99", "description": "Background:
Rust compile-time guarantees are absent in C. We must reconstitute equivalent runtime/proof guarantees via explicit mechanisms.

Goal:
Implement asx_core semantic primitives and safety scaffolding with deterministic failure behavior.

Core obligations:
- explicit state authorities
- generation-safe handles and ownership boundaries
- ghost monitors and linearity checks
- strict resource exhaustion semantics
- canonical error taxonomy and stability policy

Why this serves users:
- correctness under pressure for embedded systems
- predictable failure semantics for high-stakes systems
- confidence that performance tuning does not erase guarantees.

Cross-cutting quality contract:
- Child tasks must define and link unit tests, e2e script coverage (when behavioral), and structured log/manifest artifacts or explicit rationale for non-applicability.
- Completion evidence must remain compatible with bd-66l.9 enforcement and traceability index expectations.", "status": "closed", "priority": 0, "issue_type": "epic", "created_at": "2026-02-27T00:47:42.038789705Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-02-27T19:59:25.155834562Z", "closed_at": "2026-02-27T19:59:25.155810537Z", "close_reason": "done", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["core", "phase-3", "phase-4", "safety", "semantics"]}
